title: Why being bored is stimulating - and useful, too

sentences.label: A
sentences.en: We all know how it feels - it's impossible to keep your mind on anything, time stretches out, and all the things you could do seem equally unlikely to make you feel better.
sentences.zh: 我们都知道那种感觉：脑子无法专注于任何事，时间被拉长，而所有可做的事情似乎都同样不可能让你感觉好些。

grammar.type: 并列句 + it 形式主语
grammar.note: 破折号后展开解释 how it feels；it's impossible to do 中 it 是形式主语；and 连接三个并列现象。

sentences.label: A
sentences.en: But defining boredom so that it can be studied in the lab has proved difficult.
sentences.zh: 但是，要给无聊下定义，以便能在实验室中研究它，已经证明很困难。

grammar.type: 动名词主语 + 目的状语
grammar.note: defining boredom 是主语；so that 引导目的状语从句；has proved difficult 表示结果状态。

sentences.label: A
sentences.en: For a start, it can include a lot of other mental states, such as frustration, apathy, depression and indifference.
sentences.zh: 首先，它可能包含许多其他心理状态，如挫败、冷漠、抑郁和漠然。

grammar.note: such as 后列举 mental states 的具体类型。

sentences.label: A
sentences.en: There isn't even agreement over whether boredom is always a low-energy, flat kind of emotion or whether feeling agitated and restless counts as boredom, too.
sentences.zh: 人们甚至没有达成一致：无聊是否总是一种低能量、平淡的情绪，或者焦躁不安是否也算无聊。

grammar.note: over 后接两个 whether 从句，说明争议内容；feeling agitated and restless 是动名词短语作从句主语。

sentences.label: A
sentences.en: In his book, Boredom: A Lively History, Peter Toohey at the University of Calgary, Canada, compares it to disgust - an emotion that motivates us to stay away from certain situations.
sentences.zh: 在《无聊：一部生动的历史》中，加拿大卡尔加里大学的 Peter Toohey 把无聊比作厌恶——一种促使我们远离某些情境的情绪。

grammar.type: compare A to B + 同位解释
grammar.note: compares it to disgust 为主干；破折号后的 an emotion 解释 disgust；that 引导定语从句。

sentences.label: A
sentences.en: If disgust protects humans from infection, boredom may protect them from infectious social situations, he suggests.
sentences.zh: 他认为，如果厌恶保护人类免受感染，那么无聊也许能保护人类远离“有感染性”的社交情境。

grammar.note: If 从句提出类比前提；may protect 表推测；he suggests 是插入性报告语。

sentences.label: B
sentences.en: By asking people about their experiences of boredom, Thomas Goetz and his team at the University of Konstanz in Germany have recently identified five distinct types: indifferent, calibrating, searching, reactant and apathetic.
sentences.zh: 通过询问人们的无聊经历，德国康斯坦茨大学的 Thomas Goetz 及其团队最近识别出五种不同类型：漠然型、校准型、搜索型、反应型和冷漠型。

grammar.note: By asking... 表方法；have identified 表近期研究成果；冒号后列举五类。

sentences.label: B
sentences.en: These can be plotted on two axes - one running left to right, which measures low to high arousal, and the other from top to bottom, which measures how positive or negative the feeling is.
sentences.zh: 这些类型可以画在两条轴上：一条从左到右，衡量从低到高的唤醒程度；另一条从上到下，衡量这种感受有多积极或消极。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 分词补充
grammar.note: can be plotted 为被动；one...and the other... 解释 two axes；which 引导非限制性定语从句。

sentences.label: B
sentences.en: Intriguingly, Goetz has found that while people experience all kinds of boredom, they tend to specialise in one.
sentences.zh: 有趣的是，Goetz 发现，尽管人们会经历各种无聊，他们往往会特别偏向其中一种。

grammar.type: that 宾语从句 + while 让步
grammar.note: has found 后接宾语从句；while 表让步；tend to 表倾向。

sentences.label: B
sentences.en: Of the five types, the most damaging is reactant boredom with its explosive combination of high arousal and negative emotion.
sentences.zh: 在五种类型中，最有害的是反应型无聊，它结合了高唤醒和负面情绪，具有爆发性。

grammar.note: the most damaging 为表语；with its... 补充说明 reactant boredom 的特征。

sentences.label: B
sentences.en: The most useful is what Goetz calls indifferent boredom: someone isn't engaged in anything satisfying but still feels relaxed and calm.
sentences.zh: 最有用的是 Goetz 所说的漠然型无聊：一个人没有投入任何令人满意的事情，但仍然感到放松和平静。

grammar.type: what 名词性从句 + 冒号解释
grammar.note: what Goetz calls... 作表语；冒号后具体解释该类型。

sentences.label: B
sentences.en: However, it remains to be seen whether there are any character traits that predict the kind of boredom each of us might be prone to.
sentences.zh: 然而，是否存在某些性格特征能预测每个人容易产生哪类无聊，还有待观察。

grammar.type: it remains to be seen + whether
grammar.note: it 是形式主语；whether 从句是真正内容；that predict... 修饰 traits。

sentences.label: C
sentences.en: Psychologist Sandi Mann at the University of Central Lancashire, UK, goes further.
sentences.zh: 英国中央兰开夏大学的心理学家 Sandi Mann 则更进一步。

grammar.note: goes further 表示在前人观点基础上提出更进一步的看法。

sentences.label: C
sentences.en: All emotions are there for a reason, including boredom, she says.
sentences.zh: 她说，所有情绪的存在都有原因，包括无聊。

grammar.note: including boredom 补充说明 all emotions 的范围；she says 是报告语。

sentences.label: C
sentences.en: Mann has found that being bored makes us more creative.
sentences.zh: Mann 发现，无聊会让我们更有创造力。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + make 复合结构
grammar.note: that 引导 found 的宾语从句；being bored 作从句主语；makes us more creative 为 make + 宾语 + 宾补。

sentences.label: C
sentences.en: We're all afraid of being bored but in actual fact it can lead to all kinds of amazing things, she says.
sentences.zh: 她说，我们都害怕无聊，但事实上它能带来各种令人惊奇的事情。

grammar.note: but 连接转折；lead to 表示“导致、带来”。

sentences.label: C
sentences.en: In experiments published last year, Mann found that people who had been made to feel bored by copying numbers out of the phone book for 15 minutes came up with more creative ideas about how to use a polystyrene cup than a control group.
sentences.zh: 在去年发表的实验中，Mann 发现，那些被要求抄电话簿号码 15 分钟而感到无聊的人，比对照组想出了更多关于如何使用聚苯乙烯杯的创意。

grammar.type: 复杂宾语从句 + 比较
grammar.note: found 后是 that 从句；who 引导定语从句修饰 people；more...than 构成比较。

sentences.label: C
sentences.en: Mann concluded that a passive, boring activity is best for creativity because it allows the mind to wander.
sentences.zh: Mann 得出结论：被动而无聊的活动最有利于创造力，因为它允许思维游走。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + because 原因
grammar.note: concluded 后接 that 从句；because 解释为什么有利于创造力。

sentences.label: C
sentences.en: In fact, she goes so far as to suggest that we should seek out more boredom in our lives.
sentences.zh: 事实上，她甚至建议我们应该在生活中主动寻找更多无聊。

grammar.type: so far as to + that 从句
grammar.note: goes so far as to suggest 表“甚至建议”；that 引导建议内容。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: Psychologist John Eastwood at York University in Toronto, Canada, isn't convinced.
sentences.zh: 加拿大多伦多约克大学的心理学家 John Eastwood 并不信服。

grammar.note: isn't convinced 表示“不相信、不认同”。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: If you are in a state of mind-wandering you are not bored, he says.
sentences.zh: 他说，如果你处于思维游走状态，那你就不是无聊。

grammar.note: If 从句提出条件；主句给出判断。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: In my view, by definition boredom is an undesirable state.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，按定义来说，无聊是一种不受欢迎的状态。

grammar.note: In my view 与 by definition 均为评注状语；主干为 boredom is a state。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: That doesn't necessarily mean that it isn't adaptive, he adds.
sentences.zh: 他补充说，这并不一定意味着它没有适应性。

grammar.type: 双重否定 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: doesn't necessarily mean 表“不一定意味着”；that 引导宾语从句。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: Pain is adaptive - if we didn't have physical pain, bad things would happen to us.
sentences.zh: 疼痛是有适应性的——如果没有身体疼痛，坏事就会发生在我们身上。

grammar.note: if we didn't have... 是与现实相反的虚拟条件；would happen 为主句。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: But even if boredom has evolved to help us survive, it can still be toxic if allowed to fester.
sentences.zh: 但即使无聊进化出来是为了帮助我们生存，如果任其恶化，它仍然可能有害。

grammar.type: even if 让步 + if 省略
grammar.note: even if 引导让步；if allowed to fester 省略 it is。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: For Eastwood, the central feature of boredom is a failure to put our attention system into gear.
sentences.zh: 对 Eastwood 来说，无聊的核心特征是未能启动我们的注意系统。

grammar.type: 主系表 + 不定式修饰
grammar.note: feature is a failure 为主干；to put... 修饰 failure。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: This causes an inability to focus on anything, which makes time seem to go painfully slowly.
sentences.zh: 这会导致无法专注于任何事情，从而让时间显得慢得令人痛苦。

grammar.type: 因果 + 非限制性定语从句
grammar.note: This causes... 表结果；which 指代前句结果并继续说明影响。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: What's more, your efforts to improve the situation can end up making you feel worse.
sentences.zh: 更重要的是，你试图改善局面的努力最终可能让你感觉更糟。

grammar.note: efforts 是主语；to improve... 修饰 efforts；end up making... 表最终结果。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: People try to connect with the world and if they are not successful there's that frustration and irritability, he says.
sentences.zh: 他说，人们试图与世界建立联系，如果不成功，就会产生那种挫败和烦躁。

grammar.note: try to connect 与 if 从句构成因果说明；there's 表出现某种状态。

sentences.label: D
sentences.en: Perhaps most worryingly, says Eastwood, repeatedly failing to engage attention can lead to a state where we don't know what to do any more, and no longer care.
sentences.zh: Eastwood 说，也许最令人担忧的是，反复无法集中注意力会导致一种状态：我们不再知道该做什么，也不再在乎。

grammar.type: 动名词主语 + where 定语从句
grammar.note: repeatedly failing... 作主语；where 引导定语从句修饰 state。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: Eastwood's team is now trying to explore why the attention system fails.
sentences.zh: Eastwood 的团队现在正试图探究注意系统为什么会失灵。

grammar.note: explore 后接 why 从句，说明研究问题。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: It's early days but they think that at least some of it comes down to personality.
sentences.zh: 现在还为时尚早，但他们认为其中至少一部分归因于性格。

grammar.type: 转折并列 + that 从句
grammar.note: It's early days 表研究尚早；comes down to 表归结为。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: Boredom proneness has been linked with a variety of traits.
sentences.zh: 容易无聊已经被发现与多种特征有关。

grammar.note: has been linked with 表研究发现的关联。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: People who are motivated by pleasure seem to suffer particularly badly.
sentences.zh: 以追求愉悦为动力的人似乎尤其容易受其困扰。

grammar.note: who are motivated by pleasure 修饰 People；seem to 表推测判断。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: Other personality traits, such as curiosity, are associated with a high boredom threshold.
sentences.zh: 其他性格特征，如好奇心，则与较高的无聊阈值有关。

grammar.type: 插入列举 + 被动关联
grammar.note: such as curiosity 为例子；are associated with 表“与……相关”。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: More evidence that boredom has detrimental effects comes from studies of people who are more or less prone to boredom.
sentences.zh: 更多关于无聊具有有害影响的证据，来自对不同程度容易无聊的人群的研究。

grammar.type: 同位语从句 + 定语从句
grammar.note: that boredom has... 解释 evidence；who are... 修饰 people。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: It seems those who bore easily face poorer prospects in education, their career and even life in general.
sentences.zh: 那些容易感到无聊的人，在教育、职业甚至整体生活方面似乎前景更差。

grammar.note: those who bore easily 是从句主语；face poorer prospects 为谓语宾语。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: But of course, boredom itself cannot kill - it's the things we do to deal with it that may put us in danger.
sentences.zh: 当然，无聊本身不会致命；可能让我们陷入危险的是我们为了应对它而做的事情。

grammar.note: it's...that... 为强调结构；to deal with it 修饰 things。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: What can we do to alleviate it before it comes to that?
sentences.zh: 在事情发展到那一步之前，我们能做些什么来缓解它呢？

grammar.type: 特殊疑问句 + 不定式目的
grammar.note: What can we do 为主干；to alleviate it 表目的。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: Goetz's group has one suggestion.
sentences.zh: Goetz 的团队有一个建议。

grammar.note: has one suggestion 过渡到下文解决方案。

sentences.label: E
sentences.en: Working with teenagers, they found that those who approach a boring situation report less boredom than those who try to avoid it by using snacks, TV or social media for distraction.
sentences.zh: 他们在与青少年合作研究时发现，那些“接近”无聊情境的人，比那些通过零食、电视或社交媒体转移注意力来逃避的人报告的无聊更少。

grammar.type: 分词状语 + 比较结构
grammar.note: Working with... 为背景；that 从句为 found 的宾语；less...than 比较两类人。

sentences.label: F
sentences.en: Psychologist Francoise Wemelsfelder speculates that our over-connected lifestyles might even be a new source of boredom.
sentences.zh: 心理学家 Francoise Wemelsfelder 推测，我们过度连接的生活方式甚至可能成为无聊的新来源。

grammar.note: speculates 后接 that 从句；might 表推测。

sentences.label: F
sentences.en: In modern human society there is a lot of overstimulation but still a lot of problems finding meaning, she says.
sentences.zh: 她说，在现代人类社会中，有大量过度刺激，但人们仍然很难找到意义。

grammar.note: there is...but still... 对比刺激很多与意义不足。

sentences.label: F
sentences.en: So instead of seeking yet more mental stimulation, perhaps we should leave our phones alone, and use boredom to motivate us to engage with the world in a more meaningful way.
sentences.zh: 因此，也许我们不该继续寻求更多精神刺激，而应该放下手机，利用无聊来促使自己以更有意义的方式参与世界。

grammar.note: instead of seeking... 表取代方案；leave 与 use 为并列谓语；to motivate... 表目的。

questions.title: Questions 14-19 · Matching headings
instructions: Reading Passage 2 has six paragraphs, A-F.
Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.
i The productive outcomes that may result from boredom
ii What teachers can do to prevent boredom
iii A new explanation and a new cure for boredom
iv Problems with a scientific approach to boredom
v A potential danger arising from boredom
vi Creating a system of classification for feelings of boredom
vii Age groups most affected by boredom
viii Identifying those most affected by boredom

questions.title: Questions 20-23 · Matching features
instructions: Match each person with the correct idea, A-E.
A The way we live today may encourage boredom.
B One sort of boredom is worse than all the others.
C Levels of boredom may fall in the future.
D Trying to cope with boredom can increase its negative effects.
E Boredom may encourage us to avoid an unpleasant experience.

questions.title: Questions 24-26 · Summary completion
instructions: Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

items.prompt: People cannot ___, due to a failure in the 'attention system'.
Those for whom ___ is an important aim in life may have problems coping with boredom.
Those who have the characteristic of ___ can generally cope with it.
